# Create base controller

**Concept Source**: Based on practices from [rails-bestpractices.com](https://rails-bestpractices.com/posts/2011/06/02/create-base-conroller/)

**Original Article Date**: 02 Jun 2011

**Original Author**: Guo Lei

**Note**: This practice is rewritten in our own words based on the concept. The practice itself 
is a fact about Rails behavior and community knowledge.

## Description

Have base controllers for DRY

## Why

To keep codes clear, we usually put controllers with same business scope into the same directory. In most cases, these controllers will share some functions, so we can create a base controller for them for DRY.

For example, we have a list of controllers for content management:
